notes: First normal feeling lift 12 weeks after injured left index finger.
PR 32.5, but may need to drop weight a bit as left hand break was breaking slightly.
Slightly lower weight today but owned it.
Default working weight. Did not feel super strong.
Owned the weight. Feeling good!
First new training exercise to walk crawl up crimps. Could only make a few moves before falling. Ideally need to find slow crawl to the top. Experimentation still needed.
Havent lifted in a while but 205 felt good at 6 reps.
First progression owning this weight since finger injury on July 11.
Nearly a full crawl on the dual Tex orange edges so more of a half crimp position. Not bad.
Best lift in months at hotel gym. Fingers feeling good
Lifts felt good.
The weight feels good but think I want another session at this weight to own it fully.
Felt good.
Totally owned the weight. Ready to progress!
Weight feels more challenging on the left hand but still owned it.
Checked the box on 2nd finger training session of the week. Weight still feels harder on left hand but owned it.
10 reps. Not bad after being sick all week.
Just naked barbell slow squats 15 reps.
First time doing both sets at 35lbs. Ready to do sets at 35 and then 37.5 next session.
Took 2nd set to 37.5. Owned the weight on right hand. Left hand felt a little harder but still owned it with no break in grip or MCP flexion. Nice!
37.5 for 2 sets. Left hand felt harder on first set but better on the second set.
Both hands felt harder today. But I also did this after my climbing session and in the evening.
Overall felt good. Final lift on left hand felt challenging but maintained grip form. Can probably try 40lb next session.

First set with 35.3 kettlebell (16Kg) at 7:3. Second set at 5:3 repeaters with 40.3. Nice!
Continued using the black potato chip crimps to work my way up the wall. Did 6 or so moves and then repeated. Overall feeling better on this exercise.
First time doing this exercise in a while. Hit two working sets of 5 reps at 205 after initial set at 185.
Two sets both at 40lbs! Standard 6 reps for first set and drop it to 5 reps for second set. Can take both sets to 6 reps next session.
First session where I actually felt like I had better positioning on the crimps and got in two full bottom to top sets.
First set of 5 reps at 40. 2nd set at 42.5. Warmed up with a set at 30.
5 reps. Felt good. REP 8, probably.
Spent time thinking about the route I want to take. Working up to 3 full laps. Still need to define all the hand and foot holds though.
5 or 6 reps at this weight. Not bad

3 (mostly) laps on the wall. Still need to dial in the specific holds and movement though.
Added 10 lbs to weight belt.
Went to this weight only on the second set and for 5 reps. It felt pretty tough also not my normal set up. Extra length in my sling made things maybe feel harder.
Challenging today. Grip broke on last rep or two on the left hand. Need to make sure I’m owning this weight.
40.3 for both sets of 6 reps. Actually felt pretty challenging.
42.5 on the second set. 40 on first set.
Almost 3 laps. Grip position and no-momentum movement was better. Still need to dial-in the sequence overall.

3 full laps. Right index finger feeling tweaky and stiff, and is visibly inflamed. Might need to deload for the next or so.
New change to the protocol on these lifts going from repeaters to pure 3 second lifts for 5 reps and 3 sets. This allowed me to lift more weight than with the repeater protocol!

Back to basics starting at a 20lb partial squat to build strength up after the left knee meniscus tear.
